The 235U target, Au layer, and VYNS film thickness
The 235U target was placed at 45 deg. to the axis of
the fragment detectors and the neutron beam.
A highly collimated beam of thermal
neutrons from the 10 MW reactor HIFAR
with the beam diameter of 1.5 cm.
The neutrons emerge from the graphite reflector of the
reactor and consequently the fast neutron and
epithermal components of the beam are very small.
Typical surface barrier diodes made of n-type
silicon and operated at 90 V reverse bias.
Detectors were collimated to active areas of 3 cm2.
~60 l. of NE323, a trimethyl benzene scintillator
containing a loading of 0.5% by weight gadolinium.
Corrected for scintillator geometry and backscatter
The detector geometry and backscatter correction
(l) the excitation energies of the two fragments
(2) the detection efficiency of the scintillator was
correlated with the fragment direction) were
corrected for detector geometry and backscatter.

the scission neutrons were emitted from each fragment
